name: rn-zustand-patterns
description: Zustand state management patterns for React Native. Use when working with Zustand stores, debugging state timing issues, or implementing async actions in Zustand.
capabilities: Explain set() synchronous behavior versus React render batching
Prevent stale closures in async store actions using get()
Implement proper async actions with try/catch and state checks
Optimize selectors to avoid unnecessary re-renders
Create store subscriptions for side effects outside components
Debug state changes with devtools middleware and logging
author: CJHarmath
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing a single SKILL.md file with educational content about Zustand patterns. The static analyzer flagged 51 potential issues, but ALL are false positives. Backticks are Markdown code fences (not shell execution), and words like 'create', 'fetch', 'get', 'shallow' are JavaScript identifiers incorrectly matched as cryptographic/network terms. No executable code, scripts, network calls, file system access, or credential handling exists in this skill.
valueStatement: Zustand state management in React Native has subtle timing behaviors that cause hard-to-debug issues. This skill provides proven patterns to handle synchronous set() calls, avoid stale closures in async operations, and implement reliable store subscriptions.
userTitle: Apply Zustand patterns for React Native
limitations: Does not generate complete store implementations from requirements
Does not test or validate existing Zustand code
Does not provide runtime performance profiling
Does not cover non-React Native Zustand usage patterns

useCases.title: Debug state timing issues
useCases.description: Understand why set() updates state immediately but components show old values until re-render

useCases.title: Fix async action bugs
useCases.description: Implement async store actions that correctly read state after await without stale closure problems

useCases.title: Optimize store subscriptions
useCases.description: Set up reactive side effects that trigger on specific state changes outside React components

promptTemplates.title: Understanding set()
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain why set() is synchronous but my React Native component still shows the old value after calling set(). Include how to verify the actual state immediately after set().

promptTemplates.title: Fixing stale closures
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to fix stale closure issues in my Zustand store when I have async callbacks. After await operations, how do I ensure I am reading the current state?

promptTemplates.title: Async action patterns
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a pattern for a Zustand async action that properly handles loading state, errors, and ensures the caller can await the result. Include race condition protection.

promptTemplates.title: Selector optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: My Zustand selectors are causing too many re-renders in my React Native app. Show me how to use shallow comparison and multiple selectors to optimize performance.

bestPractices: Always use get() or getState() after any await to avoid stale closures
Use shallow comparison for object selectors to prevent unnecessary re-renders
Implement subscribe() for side effects that should run outside React components
antiPatterns: Relying on closure-captured state after async operations without re-reading with get()
Returning non-Promise results from async actions that callers try to await
Creating new objects in selectors without shallow comparison, causing excessive re-renders

faq.question: What Zustand versions does this skill cover?
faq.answer: Covers both Zustand 4.x and 5.x, including migration notes for shallow comparison and useShallow hook differences.

faq.question: Can I use these patterns in plain React?
faq.answer: Yes, most patterns apply to React web apps. Only React Native specific debugging tools differ.

faq.question: Does this skill write code to my project?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides patterns and guidance. It does not modify files or execute code.

faq.question: How do I test Zustand stores?
faq.answer: Add a reset action to your store, call getState() to access store methods directly, and reset state before each test.

faq.question: What causes selectors to trigger extra re-renders?
faq.answer: Returning new object instances from selectors breaks reference equality. Use shallow comparison or split into multiple selectors.

faq.question: How is this different from Redux patterns?
faq.answer: Zustand avoids boilerplate but requires understanding its synchronous set() behavior versus asynchronous React renders. No middleware chain exists.
